description_long: This object is a wooden pallet constructed from several parallel planks of rough, weathered wood, spaced evenly apart and supported by perpendicular beams beneath. The wood shows visible grain, knots, and signs of wear, giving it a rustic, utilitarian appearance. The pallet has three main support runners on the bottom, creating two large open slots for forklift or pallet jack access. The top surface is flat but uneven due to the natural texture and aging of the wood. The sides reveal the layered construction and the open spaces between the planks, which allow for airflow and easy handling. This type of pallet is commonly used for transporting and storing goods in warehouses, loading docks, and urban storage areas.
description: A weathered wooden pallet with spaced planks and open slots for forklift access, showing rough grain and signs of use. Its flat, sturdy surface is ideal for supporting heavy loads in urban storage or transport settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open slots beneath the pallet and the parallel arrangement of the top planks.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the rough wood texture and the three main support runners underneath.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with visible open slots and the layered plank construction.
description_view_3: Angled view similar to view 1, emphasizing the side profile and the spacing between the planks.
attribute_placement_location: floor, warehouse, loading dock, outdoor, stackable
